authority: repository source, workflow, documentation, policy, test, scenario, and evidence writes; public upstream Git-ref identity reads; read-only source audits; no authenticated GitHub setting, workflow-run, provider, environment-value, secret, deployment, message, release, approval-resume, or Production access

impactLedger.result: CI is bounded read-only, interactive Claude requires one exact owner comment beginning with @claude and read-only tools, release is a serialized version-PR writer disabled pending the exact authority epoch, and autonomous Claude review is retired.

Fifteen exact workflow, provider, and interaction records preserve principal, operation, resource, environment, duration/revocation, approval, readback, evidence, rollback, stop, escalation, and non-claim; unavailable readback remains inconclusive.
Every local action use has an exact reviewed 40-character pin, approved workflow locations, public-ref provenance, update owner, and atomic rollback requirement.

The verification gate rejects inventory, permission, timeout, convergence, target, epoch, pin, direct-invocation, 15-record classification, readback, secret, tool-authority, and emergency-containment drift with bounded diagnostics.

impactLedger.surface: Docs, READMEs, repository skill, SPEC, and active plan
impactLedger.result: Current routes point to the authority and automation owners; HGI-308 provenance, HGI-304 retirement, and HGI-306 restoration/admission ownership are coherent and maintained by executable policy.

impactLedger.surface: App and package runtime contracts, exports, React, and APIs
impactLedger.result: Runtime implementation, package exports, React, Schemas, services, Layers, HTTP APIs, and provider adapters are unchanged except for authority wording in the agent instruction and incident-runbook stop paths.

impactLedger.surface: External GitHub settings and provider state
impactLedger.result: No authenticated readback or mutation occurred. HGI-309 retains external-setting and provider application/proof; repository source makes no runtime or Production claim.

checks.result: check:authority passed exactly three workflows; all 15 authority classifications, exact action pins, direct invocation, negative drift fixtures, and emergency containment were accepted.
check:docs passed 220 files and check:skills passed 167 files with 16 mirrors.
A fresh Terra evaluator initially rejected three coherence gaps, accepted their source/test corrections, recomputed every inventory hash, and passed all six bounded scenarios.

checks.check: formatting, type-aware analysis, and lint fixtures
checks.result: Ultracite checked 528 formatting files and 203 type-aware lint files with zero findings; the lint fixture and Knip passed.
Effect setup, boundary/docs/skills/authority gates, 48 tooling tests, five package typechecks, and 250 app/package tests passed using only a schema-valid synthetic Executor configuration.

limitations: Repository source, public Git refs, local tests, and fresh-context review do not prove GitHub settings, variables, tokens, OIDC, secret availability, hosted action behavior, workflow runs, comments, pull requests, or provider state.
Release remains disabled_pending_proof; HGI-309 owns authenticated external application/readback and HGI-305 owns critical-journey proof.
HGI-306 owns continuous-automation admission and any future attempt to restore automatic review.
nonClaims: No provider, environment value, ignored local file, GitHub setting, workflow run, credential, secret, deployment, webhook, message, approval resume, pull request, release, or Production system was read or changed.
A policy pass, action pin, workflow source, public ref, old receipt, model/tool output, or unavailable readback grants no external-operation authority.
rollback: Revert workflow desired source, the three authority/automation/action owners, policy/tests/config, README/skill routes, current SPEC/plan state, and HGI-304 evidence together; preserve every previously accepted HGI receipt and all unchanged runtime implementation.
